prose:
Method provenance and result
Property: HOMO–LUMO (frontier-orbital) energy gap, eV
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) using PySCF
Geometry provenance: fixed input geometry from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/ethylene/ethylene.xyz ; single-point calculation (no re-optimization) → DFT//input-xyz
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software: PySCF (version as installed by chemkit; program field in JSON: "pyscf")
Convergence & diagnostics: SCF converged in 6 cycles; integrity status ok, trustworthy=true; no warnings reported.
Live log: the PySCF run was streamed to a frontier_YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.out file in the working directory during execution; final JSON saved to /home/erobles/ethylene_frontier.json for full details and reproducibility.

Headline result
HOMO–LUMO gap (Koopmans orbital gap): 7.4357165 eV

The value was obtained with the chemkit ‘frontier-orbitals’ skill using the flags:
  --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1
